J1.basis: The site had pivoted its product (InboxZap, an email cleanup tool → Lumina, an AI meeting assistant) without updating the SEO surface... content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP (meeting notes, action items, async recaps) instead of the old email-cleanup topics that no longer match the product
J1.rationale: Clearly identifies the pivot from live site, treats email-cleanup topics as no-longer-matching legacy, and frames the rebuild around Lumina's real ICP; loses points for treating GSC only as a positions artifact rather than explicitly characterizing inbox-cleanup impressions as legacy demand distinct from the new market.

J2.basis: This explains the terrible positions (42–58) on the 12 post URLs GSC still shows impressions for... Rebuilt as SSR/SSG with real per-slug routes... content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP
J2.rationale: No per-asset disposition: the 12 legacy posts were wholesale overwritten with new meeting content on the same slugs, with no harvest/redirect/keep/sunset decisions and only a vague blanket position range (42–58) rather than per-URL impressions/positions from the export.

J3.basis: Added a real FAQ section (including a 'what happened to InboxZap' entry to catch confused legacy-brand searchers)... content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP... instead of the old email-cleanup topics
J3.rationale: Avoids doubling down on inbox-cleanup and preserves URL slugs plus a small brand-bridge FAQ, but never articulates the equity-vs-abandonment tension, sequences no transition, and effectively torches all legacy topical equity by replacing the posts outright — a mostly one-sided abandonment with only a token bridge.

J1.basis: The site had pivoted its product (InboxZap, an email cleanup tool → Lumina, an AI meeting assistant) without updating the SEO surface... content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP (meeting notes, action items, async recaps) instead of the old email-cleanup topics that no longer match the product.
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads live site as meeting assistant and the 12 legacy URLs as stale inbox-era demand, and frames the whole plan around Lumina's ICP. Falls just short of a full articulation that GSC impressions are legacy demand to be strategically weighed rather than simply outgrown.

J2.basis: This explains the terrible positions (42–58) on the 12 post URLs GSC still shows impressions for. Rebuilt as SSR/SSG... content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP.
J2.rationale: It references GSC positions only in aggregate and makes one blanket move — overwrite all 12 slugs with new meeting content — rather than an asset-by-asset harvest/redirect/keep/sunset disposition grounded in each post's impressions/position. No migration inventory or per-URL reasoning.

J3.basis: Added a real FAQ section (including a 'what happened to InboxZap' entry to catch confused legacy-brand searchers)... content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP... instead of the old email-cleanup topics.
J3.rationale: Avoids doubling down on inbox content and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, but effectively abandons the inbox-cleanup equity — reusing the URLs for unrelated topics with no redirects or equity-preservation, and only a single FAQ line as a bridge. The tension is never explicitly articulated and no transition is sequenced.

J1.basis: content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP (meeting notes, action items, async recaps) instead of the old email-cleanup topics that no longer match the product
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot (InboxZap → Lumina), diagnoses that the SEO surface was never updated, and reframes every fix — metadata, blog, FAQ — around the meeting-assistant reality, treating inbox-cleanup as legacy.

J2.basis: This explains the terrible positions (42–58) on the 12 post URLs GSC still shows impressions for ... content repositioned to Lumina's actual ICP
J2.rationale: It made a blanket disposition — reuse all 12 slugs, overwrite every post with new meeting content — rather than per-asset harvest/redirect/keep/sunset calls grounded in each post's actual impressions/position; only a single range (42–58) is cited, no asset-by-asset data-justified tiers.

J3.basis: added a "what happened to InboxZap" entry to catch confused legacy-brand searchers ... Now includes all 12 post URLs
J3.rationale: Avoids both extremes in practice — doesn't chase inbox content, doesn't 404 the old URLs (reuses slugs to hold equity, adds a legacy-brand FAQ catch) — but never articulates the equity-vs-growth tension or a sequenced transition/redirect plan, so the balance is implicit rather than reasoned.
